Party_Details.Name: Hocen International Pte Ltd (In liquidation)

Facts.Text: Hocen International Pte Ltd was incorporated to sell and distribute power cables.
Hocen entered into four written contracts with Crossbridge between March 2006 and January 2007.
Hocen made payments totaling S$855,225 and US$52,700 via cash cheques to Crossbridge.
Hocen made bank remittances totaling US$567,200 to Crossbridge.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can claim damages for payments made without corporate benefit due to a director's actions.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interests of the company and not expose it to unjustified risks.
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff argued that the payments to Crossbridge were made without corporate benefit and were part of a scheme to procure business through improper means. The Defendant claimed the payments were for legitimate business purposes.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the payments were made as part of a scheme to procure business through improper means, but both parties were equally culpable.

Party_Details.Name: Ong Shu Lin

Facts.Text: Ong Shu Lin was appointed as a director of Hocen International Pte Ltd.
Ong Shu Lin caused Hocen to make payments to Crossbridge.
Hocen's auditor requested confirmation of payments to Crossbridge.
Ong Shu Lin executed contracts with Crossbridge on behalf of Hocen.
Ong Shu Lin applied for Hocen to be wound up on just and equitable grounds.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for payments made without corporate benefit.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must not expose the company to unjustified risks and must act in its best interests.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendant argued that the payments were for legitimate business purposes and that the Plaintiff was aware of and approved the payments. The Plaintiff claimed the payments were part of a scheme to procure business through improper means.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the Defendant breached his duty by making payments as part of a scheme to procure business through improper means.
